skill.name: commit
skill.description: Create commit messages that follow Sentry conventions, including commit types, concise subjects, body guidance, and issue references.
skill.summary: Format Sentry-style conventional commit messages with clear subjects, bodies, and issue footers.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution and a documentation URL as network behavior. Manual review found no executable code, no prompt injection, no data exfiltration, and no weak cryptographic algorithm in SKILL.md. The remaining risk is low because the skill is instructional content with one external documentation link.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The flagged lines contain Markdown code fences, inline commit types, and sample commit messages, not executable Ruby or shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown-only instructional content. No command runner, script block, or executable shell syntax appears in the flagged locations.

low_findings.title: Documentation URL Has No Runtime Network Behavior
low_findings.description: The network finding is a false positive for runtime risk. The URL points to Sentry commit message documentation in a References section and is not fetched or used by code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 131 is a Markdown reference link only. No code path exists in this skill that can make an outbound request.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Finding Not Supported
low_findings.description: The high static finding for weak cryptography is not supported by the reviewed line. Line 3 contains only the skill description and no cryptographic algorithm or hashing operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains prose about commit messages. No MD5, SHA1, encryption, hashing, or security-sensitive cryptographic use is present.

content.value_statement: Teams need commit messages that are consistent, reviewable, and easy to connect to issues. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to format Sentry-style commits with clear subjects and footers.
actual_capabilities: Formats commit headers with type, optional scope, and subject.
Applies Sentry commit types such as feat, fix, ref, perf, docs, test, build, ci, chore, style, meta, and license.
Guides concise subjects with imperative mood, capitalization, and no final period.
Adds commit bodies that explain what changed and why it matters.
Formats issue footers using Fixes or Refs for GitHub, Sentry, and Linear references.
Provides examples for fixes, features, refactors, breaking changes, and reverts.
limitations: It does not inspect git history unless the host assistant supplies that context.
It does not run git commands or create commits by itself.
It assumes Sentry conventions are the desired project standard.
It cannot verify whether referenced issue IDs are valid.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Commit Before Review
use_cases.description: Create a clear commit message from a summary of local code changes.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Commit History
use_cases.description: Apply consistent Sentry-style types, subjects, bodies, and issue references across a repository.

use_cases.title: Document Release-Relevant Changes
use_cases.description: Write commit bodies that explain motivation and link related issues without excessive detail.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Commit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a short change summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a Sentry-style commit message for this change: [describe the change]. Use the best commit type and keep the subject concise.

prompt_templates.title: Add Issue Footer
prompt_templates.scenario: Commit message with a known issue reference.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Sentry-style commit message for [change summary]. Include the issue footer [Fixes or Refs] [issue ID].

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite Existing Message
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve a draft commit message before committing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this draft as a Sentry-style commit message. Keep the meaning, improve the subject, and add a useful body if needed: [draft message].

prompt_templates.title: Split Complex Changes
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a mixed change set.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these changes and propose separate Sentry-style commit messages for each independent change: [change list]. Include issue footers where appropriate.

output_examples.input: Change: the dashboard crashed when a deleted user had no profile data. Issue: SENTRY-5678.
output_examples.output:
fix(dashboard): Handle deleted users without profile data

The dashboard could crash when rendering a deleted user with missing profile data. Add a null-safe path before reading profile fields.

Fixes SENTRY-5678

output_examples.input: Change: move duplicate validation logic from three endpoints into one shared validator.
output_examples.output:
ref: Extract shared endpoint validation

Move repeated validation logic into a shared validator so endpoint behavior stays consistent. No behavior change.

output_examples.input: Change: remove deprecated v1 API endpoints. This breaks old clients. Issue: GH-1234.
output_examples.output:
feat(api)!: Remove deprecated v1 endpoints

Remove v1 endpoints that were previously deprecated. Clients should migrate to v2 endpoints before upgrading.

BREAKING CHANGE: v1 endpoints are no longer available
Fixes GH-1234

best_practices: Provide the actual change summary, affected scope, and issue reference when asking for a commit message.
Keep each commit focused on one stable, independently reviewable change.
Use the body to explain motivation and user impact, not a full implementation log.
anti_patterns: Do not combine unrelated fixes and features into one commit message.
Do not use vague subjects such as update code or fix stuff.
Do not add Fixes footers unless the commit should close the issue when merged.

faq.question: Does this skill create git commits?
faq.answer: No. It formats commit messages. The host assistant or user must run any git commands separately.

faq.question: What commit format does it use?
faq.answer: It uses a conventional commit style with Sentry-specific guidance for types, scopes, subjects, bodies, and footers.

faq.question: Can it include GitHub issue references?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports footers such as Fixes GH-1234, Fixes #1234, and Refs GH-1234.

faq.question: Can it handle breaking changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can mark breaking changes with an exclamation point in the header and a BREAKING CHANGE footer.

faq.question: Does it validate issue IDs?
faq.answer: No. It can format issue IDs that you provide, but it does not verify them against external systems.

faq.question: Is this only for Sentry repositories?
faq.answer: It is designed for Sentry conventions, but teams with similar conventional commit rules can adapt the output.
